title: How efficient is symbiont-mediated protection to heat under daily fluctuating thermal environments? A study on an aphid-bacterium mutualism
author: Corentin Iltis
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
These are the two datasets for the article " How efficient is symbiont-mediated protection to heat under daily fluctuating thermal environments? A study on an aphid-bacterium mutualism ". 

 We examined the effects of four thermal regimes defined by their combination of mean temperature (18 or 25°C) and diurnal thermal range (day-night fluctuations: 5 or 10°C) on the interaction between the aphid  Acyrthosiphon pisum , its obligate symbiont  Buchnera aphidicola  and the facultative heat-protective symbiont  Serratia symbiotica . We used two clonal lines of  A. pisum  uninfected (5A) or infected with  S. symbiotica  (5AR). Thermal treatments were applied form aphid birth to death. We recorded both aphid traits (immature survival rate, longevity, immature development time, total fecundity, body size) and symbiont titres at the onset of reproduction (for both  B. aphidicola  and  S. symbiotica ), expressed relatively to aphid gene copy number through quantitative PCRs (qPCRs). 

  Aphid traits  

 . Individual = identification of the individual 

 . Line = aphid clonal line, either deprived of (5A) or infected with  S. symbiotica  (5AR) 

 . MT = Mean Temperature, either 18°C or 25°C 

 . DTR = Diurnal Temperature Range (day-night fluctuations), either 5°C or 10°C 

 . surv.adult = whether (Y) or not (N) the individual survived until reaching reproduction (immature survival rate) 

 . adulthood = time elapsed between birth and first reproduction (in days) 

 . lifespan = time elapsed between birth and death (in days) 

 . offspring = total number of larvae produced by each aphid throughout lifetime 

 . tibia = hind tibia length as a proxy of body size (in mm) 

 . wing = whether (Y) or not (N) the individual developed wings following imaginal moult 

   

  Symbiont titres  

 . Individual = identification of the individual 

 . Line = aphid clonal line, either deprived of (A) or infected with  S. symbiotica  (AR) 

 . MT = Mean Temperature, either 18°C or 25°C 

 . DTR = Diurnal Temperature Range (day-night fluctuations), either 5°C or 10°C 

 . QPCR = identification of the microplate used during qPCR analyses (to handle pseudoreplication) 

 . EfAlph = number of copies of aphid housekeeping gene elongation factor 1-alpha ( EF1-α ) 

 . SdEfAlph = standard deviation of the aphid gene copy number, calculated over three technical replicates 

 . DnaKBa = number of copies of  B. aphidicola  gene  dnaK  

 . SdDnaKBa = standard deviation of  B. aphidicola  gene copy number, calculated over three technical replicates 

 . DnakSs = number of copies of  S. symbiotica  gene  dnaK  

 . SdDnaKSs = standard deviation of  S. symbiotica  gene copy number, calculated over three technical replicates 

 . RappBa = ratio between DnaKBa and EfAlph to calculate relative titre of  B. aphidicola  

 . RappSs = ratio between DnaKSs and EfAlph to calculate relative titre of  S. symbiotica
